Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log for customer support tickets We need to identify cases with significantly longer resolution times determine potential root causes of the delays and explain how these factors lead to delays and propose recommendations

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: First we will calculate the total resolution time for each case from Receive Ticket to Close Ticket Then we can compare the times to identify outliers

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets compute the duration for each case Case 101 Duration 2 hours and 15 minutes Case 102 total 25 hours 10 minutes Case 103 Duration 1 hour 20 minutes Case 104 total 24 hours 10 minutes Case 105 total 49 hours 5 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now lets list the durations 101 2h15m 102 25h10m 103 1h20m 104 24h10m 105 49h5m We can see that cases 102 104 and 105 have much longer resolution times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Well break down each of these slow cases to see what happened

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102 Events Receive Ticket 0805 Triage Ticket 0830 Assign to Level 1 Agent 0900 Escalate to Level 2 Agent 1130 Investigate Issue 1400 Resolve Ticket next day 0900 Close Ticket 0915

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The escalation took a long time and there is a 2h30m gap after escalation and before investigation Then a long gap from investigation 1400 to resolution next day 0900 which is 19 hours

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This might be because the level 2 agent didnt start immediately or the ticket was not picked up until the next day

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 104 Events Receive Ticket 0820 Triage Ticket 0900 Assign to Level 1 Agent 0930 Investigate Issue 1300 Resolve Ticket next day at 0800 and closed at 0830

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After assignment 0930 it took until 1300 3h30m for the agent to start investigation Then after investigation 1300 the ticket was not resolved until the next day at 0800 19 hours later

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This might be because the agent didnt work on it until the next day

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 105 Events Receive Ticket 0825 Triage Ticket 0835 Assign to Level 1 Agent 0900 Investigate Issue 0910 Escalate to Level 2 Agent 1000 Investigate Issue at 1400 on day 2 resolved at 0900 on day 3 and closed at 0930

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After escalation at 1000 on day 1 there was a huge delay over 24 hours until the Level 2 agent started the investigation on day 2 at 1400 Then from investigation at 1400 on day 2 to resolution at 0900 on day 3 is 19 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Potential Root Causes Delays in escalation handling In case 102 and 105 after escalation there was a significant delay before the Level 2 agent started the investigation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Long waiting time for assignment or investigation In case 104 the assignment to Level 1 took 3 point 5 hours to start investigation after assignment In case 102 there were multiple delays

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Overnight delays In several cases there are gaps that span the overnight period which might be due to agents working during business hours only

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Or perhaps the issue was complex and took time

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In case 101 and 103 we did not have escalations and the entire process was done within the same day Also the investigation duration in case 101 was 1 hour and in 103 was 30 minutes

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests that the agent might have set the ticket aside and not worked on it until the next business day

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Escalation to Level 2 Agent might be a bottleneck The time between escalation and when the Level 2 agent starts working on it can be very long In case 105 it took over 24 hours

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Monitor and reduce the time between escalation and when the escalated ticket is picked up by the Level 2 team This might involve setting SLAs for escalation handling or having a dedicated resource for escalated tickets

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For Level 1 agents ensure that once a ticket is assigned they start working on it promptly There might be a need for training or better workload management to avoid long queues

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For overnight delays consider the possibility of extending support hours or having shift handovers to avoid tickets being left untouched overnight

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Analyze the root cause of escalations to see if there are opportunities for Level 1 agents to resolve more issues without escalation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Implement better tracking of the time each ticket spends in each state to identify systemic delays Consider a follow up process if a ticket hasnt been updated for a certain period an alert could be sent

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: If the delay in investigation to resolution is due to waiting for customer response then set clear timelines for customer responses and have an automated reminder system

Name: Validation - IND
Text: However note that the event log does not capture the reason for delays like waiting for customer input

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Summary of cases with long resolution times Case 102 25h10m Case 104 24h10m Case 105 49h5m The main factors we see are escalations leading to long waiting times long gaps between assignment and investigation and overnight delays
